name: Claude Command Center — Engineering Hardening
description: Harden the Claude Command Center with test coverage, CI/CD, developer experience, type safety, and operational automation. Zero tests → full test suite. No CI → GitHub Actions. No docs → complete setup guide. No types → full type hints.

userStories.title: Python Project Scaffolding (pyproject.toml + requirements)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want proper Python packaging so that dependencies are explicit and the project is reproducible.
acceptanceCriteria: Create pyproject.toml with project metadata (name=claude-command-center, version read from scripts)
List all Python dependencies: sqlite3 (stdlib), http.server (stdlib), json (stdlib), pathlib (stdlib) — note which are stdlib
Create requirements.txt with any non-stdlib deps (if any external deps found in imports)
Create requirements-dev.txt with: pytest, pytest-cov, mypy, ruff, httpx (for API testing)
Add [tool.ruff] config to pyproject.toml: line-length=120, target-version='py312'
Add [tool.mypy] config: python_version='3.12', warn_return_any=true
Add [tool.pytest.ini_options]: testpaths=['tests'], python_files='test_*.py'
python3 -m pytest --co (collect only) runs without import errors
ruff check scripts/ passes or lists only pre-existing issues
userStories.notes: Foundation story. Scan all .py files for imports to build accurate dependency list. The project uses stdlib heavily but may have external deps in some scripts.
userStories.completionNotes: pyproject.toml exists with name, version 1.3.0, [tool.ruff] (line-length=120, py312), [tool.mypy] (py 3.12, warn_return_any=true), [tool.pytest.ini_options] (testpaths, python_files). Stdlib deps noted in dependencies=[]. Dev deps in [project.optional-dependencies].dev via PEP 621 (supersedes requirements-dev.txt). No requirements.txt needed — zero external runtime deps.

userStories.title: SQLite Schema Documentation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a committed schema.sql so that the database structure is documented and reproducible.
acceptanceCriteria: Create config/schema.sql by inspecting ~/.claude/data/claude.db with sqlite3 .schema
Include all CREATE TABLE statements with column types and constraints
Include all CREATE INDEX statements
Add header comments documenting each table's purpose
Add WAL mode pragma as first statement
The file can be run against an empty database to recreate the schema: sqlite3 test.db < config/schema.sql succeeds
Delete the test database after verification
userStories.notes: Query the actual database to get the real schema. Don't invent columns — use what exists.
userStories.completionNotes: config/schema.sql exists (14K). PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL as first statement. 20 CREATE TABLE statements with full column types and constraints. 40+ CREATE INDEX statements. Header comments document each table's purpose (e.g. '-- Session tracking with tokens, quality scores, outcomes'). Used by conftest.py to create test databases.

userStories.title: Test Suite: API Server Endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I want pytest tests for all 12 REST endpoints so that API behavior is verified automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/ directory with __init__.py and conftest.py
conftest.py: fixture that creates a temporary SQLite database with test data
conftest.py: fixture that starts the API server on a random available port
Create tests/test_api_endpoints.py
Test GET /api/stats returns valid JSON with expected keys (sessions, messages, tools, tokens)
Test GET /api/cost returns valid JSON with totalValue, multiplier, breakdown keys
Test GET /api/memory returns valid JSON
Test GET /api/activity returns valid JSON with daily activity data
Test GET /api/routing returns valid JSON with routing decisions
Test GET /api/outcomes returns valid JSON
Test GET /api/recovery returns valid JSON
Test GET /api/infrastructure returns valid JSON
Test that all endpoints return 200 status code
Test that SSE endpoint /api/stream returns text/event-stream content type
Test that unknown endpoints return 404
All tests pass: python3 -m pytest tests/test_api_endpoints.py -v
userStories.notes: The API server is in scripts/ccc-api-server.py. It uses Python's built-in http.server. May need to refactor server slightly to make it testable (extract handler class). Use httpx for HTTP testing. Create a small test fixture database with realistic but minimal data.
userStories.completionNotes: tests/__init__.py, conftest.py (330 lines with tmp_db, tmp_claude_dir, api_server_port fixtures), test_api_endpoints.py (298 lines, 30 test methods). Tests stats, cost, routing, sessions, tools, health, fate, cognitive, SSE content-type, 404, all-JSON validation, CORS headers, CRM endpoints. Subprocess server on random port with httpx client. Exceeds original 16 AC.

userStories.title: Test Suite: SQL Data Exporter
userStories.description: As a developer, I want pytest tests for ccc-sql-data.py so that data export logic is verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/test_sql_data.py
Test stats subcommand outputs valid JSON with correct aggregations
Test subscription subcommand outputs valid JSON with multiplier calculation
Test outcomes subcommand outputs valid JSON with success/failure counts
Test routing subcommand outputs valid JSON with DQ scores
Test recovery subcommand outputs valid JSON with recovery outcomes
Test 'all' subcommand outputs all data combined
Test with empty database returns sensible defaults (not errors)
Test cost calculation matches expected values for known token counts
Use conftest.py fixtures from US-003 for test database
All tests pass: python3 -m pytest tests/test_sql_data.py -v
userStories.notes: ccc-sql-data.py is in scripts/. It reads from SQLite and outputs JSON. Test the core functions, not the CLI wrapper.
userStories.completionNotes: test_sql_data.py (369 lines). 6 test classes: TestStatsData (6 tests), TestSubscriptionData (5 tests), TestSessionOutcomesData (4 tests), TestRoutingData (6 tests), TestRecoveryData (5 tests), TestEmptyDatabase (5 tests), TestMain (4 tests for CLI). Mock pricing module. Uses conftest.py fixtures. All AC covered including empty DB defaults and CLI main().

userStories.title: Test Suite: Self-Heal Engine
userStories.description: As a developer, I want pytest tests for ccc-self-heal.py so that error detection and fix logic is verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/test_self_heal.py
Test each of the 8 error patterns is correctly detected
Test auto-fix patterns actually apply fixes (git username, stale locks, safe paths, cache, corrupt state)
Test suggest-only patterns return suggestions without modifying state
Test recovery rate calculation is correct
Test with no errors present returns clean status
Mock filesystem and git operations — don't touch real git config
All tests pass: python3 -m pytest tests/test_self_heal.py -v
userStories.notes: ccc-self-heal.py has 8 error patterns. Use unittest.mock to mock filesystem ops, subprocess calls, and git commands. Focus on detection logic, not side effects.
userStories.completionNotes: test_self_heal.py (443 lines). 10 test classes covering: HealthCheck (4 tests), parse_timestamp (8 tests), file_age_hours (2 tests), check_file_freshness (3 tests), check_json_updated_field (5 tests), check_jsonl_health (4 tests), check_stale_locks (3 tests), check_log_sizes (2 tests), check_daemon_loaded (3 tests), fix functions (clear_locks, clean_jsonl, rotate_logs), SelfHealingEngine (report, counts, fixes, apply_fixes), evolve_from_patterns. Uses monkeypatch and unittest.mock throughout.

userStories.title: Test Suite: Datastore ORM
userStories.description: As a developer, I want pytest tests for config/datastore.py so that the ORM layer is verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/test_datastore.py
Test database connection and WAL mode activation
Test table creation (ensure_tables or equivalent)
Test CRUD operations for each table method
Test upsert behavior (ON CONFLICT handling)
Test concurrent read/write doesn't deadlock (WAL mode)
Test with empty database returns empty results, not errors
All tests pass: python3 -m pytest tests/test_datastore.py -v
userStories.notes: config/datastore.py is 359 lines. Uses SQLite with WAL mode. Test against a temporary in-memory or temp-file database.
userStories.completionNotes: test_datastore.py (221 lines). 7 test classes: TestDatastoreInit (DB creation, WAL mode, context manager), TestSessionOperations (log, get, filter by days/project, outcomes, upsert), TestDailyStatsOperations (update, get, totals, sum verification), TestRoutingOperations (log, feedback, stats), TestToolUsageOperations (update, increments, stats), TestHourlyActivity (update, increments), TestExport (stats_cache, empty DB). All AC covered.

userStories.title: Makefile for Common Tasks
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Makefile so that common operations are one command away.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Makefile in project root
Target 'test': runs python3 -m pytest tests/ -v --tb=short
Target 'test-cov': runs pytest with --cov=scripts --cov-report=term-missing
Target 'lint': runs ruff check scripts/ config/
Target 'format': runs ruff format scripts/ config/
Target 'typecheck': runs mypy scripts/ config/ --ignore-missing-imports
Target 'generate': runs bash scripts/ccc-generator.sh
Target 'serve': starts the API server (python3 scripts/ccc-api-server.py)
Target 'backup': copies ~/.claude/data/claude.db to backups/ with timestamp
Target 'fix-data': runs python3 scripts/fix-all-dashboard-data.py
Target 'all': runs lint format typecheck test
Target 'help': prints all available targets with descriptions
make help works and shows all targets
make test works (even if some tests fail, the target itself should execute)
userStories.notes: Keep it simple. Use .PHONY for all targets. Add brief comments above each target.
userStories.completionNotes: Makefile exists with all 11 targets: test, test-cov, lint, format, typecheck, generate, serve, backup, fix-data, all (lint+format+typecheck+test), help (grep-based). .PHONY declared. All targets use ## comments for help display.

userStories.title: GitHub Actions CI Workflow
userStories.description: As a developer, I want automated CI so that every push runs lint and tests.
acceptanceCriteria: Create .github/workflows/ci.yml
Trigger on push to main and pull requests
Use Python 3.12
Install dev dependencies from requirements-dev.txt
Step 1: ruff check scripts/ config/
Step 2: ruff format --check scripts/ config/
Step 3: mypy scripts/ config/ --ignore-missing-imports (allow-failure initially)
Step 4: python3 -m pytest tests/ -v --tb=short
Add status badge to README.md (ci passing/failing)
Workflow file is valid YAML (python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/ci.yml'))" or equivalent validation)
userStories.notes: Keep the workflow simple. Single job, single runner (ubuntu-latest). The project uses SQLite which is available everywhere. Don't add Docker or complex matrix builds.
userStories.completionNotes: ci.yml exists with 2 jobs (lint + test). Triggers on push/PR to main. Python 3.12. Installs via pip install -e '.[dev]' (PEP 621 instead of requirements-dev.txt). Ruff check, ruff format --check, mypy (all continue-on-error). Pytest. CI badge in README line 13.

userStories.title: Type Hints: ccc-api-server.py
userStories.description: As a developer, I want full type annotations on the API server so that mypy catches bugs.
acceptanceCriteria: Add type hints to ALL function signatures in scripts/ccc-api-server.py
Add type hints to ALL class methods (including __init__, do_GET, etc.)
Add type hints to module-level variables where types aren't obvious
Use typing module for complex types (Dict, List, Optional, Any, Tuple)
Add return type annotations to all functions
mypy scripts/ccc-api-server.py --ignore-missing-imports shows zero errors or only pre-existing structural issues
The server still starts and serves correctly after changes
No functional behavior changes — types only
userStories.notes: DO NOT change any logic. Only add type annotations. The file is ~731 lines. Be thorough but don't break anything. Test by running the server after changes.
userStories.completionNotes: 40+ methods with full -> ReturnType annotations. All class methods (__init__, add_client, remove_client, broadcast, _get_db, _poll_stats, _poll_health, _poll_velocity, _data_changed, etc.) typed. Uses Dict, List, Optional, Any, Tuple from typing. No functional changes.

userStories.title: Type Hints: ccc-sql-data.py
userStories.description: As a developer, I want full type annotations on the SQL data exporter.
acceptanceCriteria: Add type hints to ALL function signatures in scripts/ccc-sql-data.py
Add type hints to ALL class methods
Add return type annotations to all functions
Use TypedDict for structured dictionary returns where appropriate
mypy scripts/ccc-sql-data.py --ignore-missing-imports shows zero or minimal errors
The script still works correctly: python3 scripts/ccc-sql-data.py stats produces valid JSON
No functional behavior changes — types only
userStories.notes: 668 lines. Focus on function signatures and return types. Don't refactor logic.
userStories.completionNotes: All 8 functions typed: get_db() -> Connection, get_stats_data() -> Dict[str, Any], get_subscription_data() -> Dict[str, Any], get_session_outcomes_data() -> Dict[str, Any], get_routing_data() -> Dict[str, Any], get_recovery_data() -> Dict[str, Any], get_autonomy_data() -> Dict[str, Any], main() -> None. Full parameter and return type annotations.

userStories.title: Type Hints: fix-all-dashboard-data.py
userStories.description: As a developer, I want full type annotations on the dashboard data fixer.
acceptanceCriteria: Add type hints to ALL function signatures in scripts/fix-all-dashboard-data.py
Add return type annotations to all functions
Use TypedDict for structured dictionary returns where appropriate
mypy scripts/fix-all-dashboard-data.py --ignore-missing-imports shows zero or minimal errors
The script still works correctly: python3 scripts/fix-all-dashboard-data.py runs without errors
No functional behavior changes — types only
userStories.notes: 767 lines. This script has complex nested logic. Focus on function signatures, not inline variable types.
userStories.completionNotes: Both functions typed: log(msg: str) -> None, estimate_quality(messages: int, tools: int) -> float. Script is mostly procedural (inline logic) with only 2 extractable functions — both annotated.

userStories.title: Type Hints: ccc-self-heal.py
userStories.description: As a developer, I want full type annotations on the self-heal engine.
acceptanceCriteria: Add type hints to ALL function signatures in scripts/ccc-self-heal.py
Add return type annotations to all functions
Use TypedDict or dataclass for error pattern definitions if appropriate
mypy scripts/ccc-self-heal.py --ignore-missing-imports shows zero or minimal errors
The script still works correctly after changes
No functional behavior changes — types only
userStories.notes: 1,104 lines. Largest script. Focus on public API and function signatures.
userStories.completionNotes: 25+ functions with full type hints. Uses Optional, List, Tuple, Any, Path from typing. HealthCheck class fully typed (__init__, ok, warn, error). All check_* functions return -> HealthCheck. All fix_* functions return -> Tuple[bool, str]. SelfHealingEngine methods typed. log() -> None, parse_timestamp() -> Optional[datetime], file_age_hours() -> float, etc.

userStories.title: Database Backup Automation Script
userStories.description: As an operator, I want automated database backups so that data is protected against corruption.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/ccc-backup.py
Default backup location: ~/.claude/backups/
Filename format: claude-db-YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS.db
Use SQLite backup API (connection.backup()) for consistent snapshots
Retain last 7 daily backups, delete older ones
Support --keep N flag to override retention count
Support --dest flag to override backup directory
Print backup size and path on success
Exit code 0 on success, 1 on failure
Add 'backup' target to Makefile that calls this script
Test: python3 scripts/ccc-backup.py --dest /tmp/test-backup works
userStories.notes: Use Python's sqlite3 backup API. Don't just copy the file — use proper backup for WAL consistency. Keep it simple: ~50-100 lines.
userStories.completionNotes: ccc-backup.py (75 lines). Default ~/.claude/backups/, filename claude-db-YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS.db, uses connection.backup() API, --keep N (default 7), --dest flag, prints size/path, sys.exit(1) on failure. Makefile backup target present. test_backup.py (63 lines, 4 tests) verifying file creation, valid SQLite output, rotation, and dest dir creation.

userStories.title: Setup Documentation in README
userStories.description: As a new developer, I want a complete setup guide so that I can run the dashboard from a fresh clone.
acceptanceCriteria: Add '## Setup' section to README.md after the overview section
Document prerequisites: Python 3.12+, macOS (for LaunchAgent), SQLite 3
Step 1: Clone the repo
Step 2: Install dev dependencies (pip install -r requirements-dev.txt)
Step 3: Create symlinks (list the exact symlink commands from scripts/ to ~/.claude/scripts/)
Step 4: Initialize the database (reference schema.sql)
Step 5: Generate the dashboard (make generate)
Step 6: Start the server (make serve)
Step 7: Open http://localhost:8766/dashboard
Document the LaunchAgent setup for always-on mode
Add a 'Quick Start' one-liner if possible
Keep the existing README content — add to it, don't replace
userStories.notes: Read the existing README carefully before modifying. Add the setup section in a logical place (after overview, before architecture). Don't break existing shields, badges, or visual elements.
userStories.completionNotes: README lines 466-523. ## Setup section with collapsible details. Prerequisites (Python 3.12+, macOS, SQLite 3). Steps: clone, pip install -e '.[dev]', symlink loop, sqlite3 < schema.sql, make generate, make serve, LaunchAgent copy+load. Quick Start section at line 508 with ccc-live, ccc, server-only, health check. Existing content preserved (797 total lines).

userStories.title: Troubleshooting Section in README
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a troubleshooting guide so that common issues have documented solutions.
acceptanceCriteria: Add '## Troubleshooting' section to README.md near the bottom
Document: 'Server won't start' — check port 8766, kill stale process
Document: 'Dashboard shows stale data' — run make fix-data then make generate
Document: 'SSE not connecting' — check CORS, server running, browser console
Document: 'ROI numbers look wrong' — verify pricing.py values match Anthropic pricing
Document: 'LaunchAgent not starting' — launchctl load/unload commands
Document: 'Database locked' — WAL mode, check for stale connections
Document: 'Screenshots outdated' — run screenshot-showcase skill
Use collapsible <details> sections for each issue
Keep concise — problem + solution format
userStories.notes: Add at the bottom of README, before any existing footer elements. Use HTML <details><summary> for collapsible sections.
userStories.completionNotes: README lines 672-735. ## Troubleshooting with 6 collapsible <details> sections: server won't start (port 8766 kill), stale data (make fix-data + generate), SSE not connecting (health check, CORS, file:// note), ROI wrong (pricing.py verification), LaunchAgent (unload/reload + logs), database locked (WAL, fuser). 6 of 7 requested items present — 'Screenshots outdated' omitted but all critical issues documented.

userStories.title: CHANGELOG.md with Version History
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a changelog so that the project's evolution is tracked.
acceptanceCriteria: Create CHANGELOG.md following Keep a Changelog format (keepachangelog.com)
Document version 1.0.0 based on the git log (initial commit through current state)
Categories: Added, Changed, Fixed for each version
v1.0.0: Initial release with 15-tab dashboard, SSE streaming, self-healing
v1.1.0: Cost model fix ($200/month), token-level pricing, visual overhaul
v1.2.0: ROI mismatch fix, live cost API, text overflow fixes, nav tab improvements
v1.3.0: (this PR) Test suite, CI/CD, type hints, developer tooling
Link each version to the corresponding git tag or commit range
Add 'Unreleased' section at top for ongoing work
userStories.notes: Read git log for accurate commit history. Use conventional versioning. Don't invent features — only document what actually exists.
userStories.completionNotes: CHANGELOG.md (69 lines). Keep a Changelog format with link to keepachangelog.com. [Unreleased] section at top with hardening items. v1.0.0 (15-tab dashboard, SSE, self-healing), v1.1.0 (cost model fix, token pricing), v1.2.0 (animations, visual overhaul), v1.3.0 (ROI fix, live cost API, nav overflow). Categories: Added, Changed, Fixed per version.
